title: Remembering Braves slugger Bob Horner
description:
We are saddened by the passing of former Braves third baseman Bob Horner.

The first overall pick in the 1978 MLB Draft, Horner made the jump straight to the Majors without playing a single day in the Minors.

Just ten days after being drafted, Horner made his MLB debut and homered off future Hall of Famer Bert Blyleven. Horner went on to blast 23 home runs in just 89 games and won NL Rookie of the Year honors.

He went on to top the 30-homer mark three times in the next four years and was a National League All-Star in 1982, when he helped lead the Atlanta Braves to a division title.

Horner spent 9 of his 10 Major League seasons with the Braves. He made history on July 6, 1986 when he slugged a record-tying 4 home runs in one game. It was the only four-homer game of the 1980s.

Horner completed his college career at Arizona State with the most home runs in NCAA history, a mark since broken. He was named MVP of the 1977 College World Series and was the very first winner of the prestigious Golden Spikes Award in 1978.

He was 68 years old.

title: Wemby’s MONSTER Game 4 Performance Ties the WCF 😳 | May 24, 2026
description:
Victor Wembanyama delivered another superstar playoff performance to help the Spurs even the Western Conference Finals at 2-2.

Wemby finished with:
🔥 33 PTS
🔥 8 REB
🔥 5 AST
🔥 3 BLK
🔥 3 3PM

With the performance, Wembanyama became just the THIRD player in NBA history to average 30+ PPG and 10+ RPG through his first four Conference Finals games.

The only others to do it:
• Hakeem Olajuwon
• Kareem Abdul-Jabbar

San Antonio’s defense also made history, holding Oklahoma City to just 82 points — the Thunder’s lowest-scoring game, regular season or playoffs, since December 2, 2021.

The series is now tied 2-2 heading into Game 5 on Tuesday night in Oklahoma City.

title: The TOP Plays of the Knicks 10-Game Playoff Win Streak 🔥
description:
The New York Knicks are making history.

With tonight’s win, New York has now won 10 consecutive playoff games — the longest postseason winning streak in franchise history — and now holds a commanding 3-0 lead in the Eastern Conference Finals.

During the streak, the Knicks have completely dominated opponents:

🔥 122.2 PPG
🔥 Only 99.7 PPG allowed
🔥 +22.5 average margin of victory

That +22.5 differential is officially the HIGHEST average margin of victory over any 10-game playoff winning streak in NBA history.

Totals during the streak:
• 1,222 PTS scored
• 997 PTS allowed
• +225 overall differential

The previous record belonged to the 2016-17 Warriors, who posted a +17.1 margin during part of their historic championship run.

Overall this postseason, New York is now:
• +234 across 13 playoff games
• +18.0 average scoring differential

The Knicks are now just one win away from their first NBA Finals appearance since 1999 and the 9th Finals trip in franchise history.

Game 4: Monday at 8 PM ET on ESPN.

🚨 The Knicks Closed Game 1 on a HISTORIC 44-11 Run

Trailing by 22 points with 7:45 remaining in the 4th quarter, the Knicks stormed back to defeat the Cavaliers 115-104 in overtime to take a 1-0 series lead.

New York closed the game on a dominant 44-11 run, completing the largest 4th-quarter comeback in Conference Finals history in the play-by-play era.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Is The 2025-26 @kia NBA Most Valuable Player!
description:
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had a year of firsts.

On Sunday, he made history with a second.

After a season that saw SGA place himself among the game’s all-time greats – or, in some cases, in a tier all by himself – the Thunder guard just became the 7th player this century and 14th ever to win back-to-back Kia NBA MVP Awards.

Joining Nikola Jokić, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Stephen Curry, LeBron James (2x), Tim Duncan and fellow Canadian Steve Nash as back-to-back winners since 2000, the two-time reigning Kia NBA MVP now leads the Thunder against the Spurs in the Western Conference Finals.

But first, how did SGA bring his game to another level in 2025-26? He…

▪️ Set the NBA record for most consecutive 20+ pt games, breaking Wilt Chamberlain’s record
▪️ Became the first player since Wilt (1963-64) with 20+ pts in every regular season game, with a minimum of 50 games
▪️ Joined Michael Jordan as the only guards with at least 30+ PPG on 50+ FG% in four straight years
▪️ Became the only guard in NBA history to average 30+ PPG on 55%+ shooting
▪️ Led the league in total Clutch points, earning Kia NBA Clutch Player of the Year honors
▪️ Had the highest +/- in the NBA (+788) by more than 100 points

title: Victor Wembanyama Becomes 3rd Youngest Ever With 25/15/5 in a Playoff Game | May 12, 2026
description:
Victor Wembanyama continues to make NBA playoff history.

The Spurs star finished with:
• 27 PTS
• 17 REB (playoff career-high)
• 5 AST
• 3 BLK
• 9-16 FG

At 22 years, 128 days old, Wembanyama became the 3rd youngest player in NBA history to record 25+ PTS, 15+ REB, and 5+ AST in a playoff game.

The only players younger to accomplish the feat:
• Magic Johnson (20 years, 276 days)
• Luka Dončić (21 years, 177 days)

Another historic postseason performance from one of the NBA’s brightest young stars.
